Markdown Edition мқёкіјнҡЁкіј м¶”лЎ  (Causal Inference)
Contents
м ң1л¶Җ | мқёкіјмқҙлЎ мқҳ кё°ліё к°ңл…җ

  • 1.1 мғҒкҙҖкіј мқёкіјмқҳ к·јліём Ғ м°Ёмқҙ - мҷң нҡҢк·Җл§ҢмңјлЎңлҠ” л¶ҖмЎұн•ңк°Җ
  • 1.2 л°ҳмӮ¬мӢӨкіј мһ мһ¬кІ°кіј - мқёкіјнҡЁкіјлҘј м–ҙл–»кІҢ м •мқҳн•  кІғмқёк°Җ
  • 1.3 мқёкіј мӮ¬лӢӨлҰ¬мҷҖ кө¬мЎ° мқёкіј лӘЁнҳ• вҖ” м„ё мёөмң„мқҳ нҳ•мӢқнҷ”
  • 1.4 м„ұлі„мқҖ мӣҗмқёмқҙ лҗ  мҲҳ м—ҶлҠ”к°Җ вҖ” л№„нҢҗм Ғ кІҖнҶ 
  • 2 мһ мһ¬кІ°кіј н”„л Ҳмһ„мӣҢнҒ¬
  • 2.1 лӢЁмң„ В· мІҳм№ҳ В· мһ мһ¬кІ°кіјмқҳ м •мқҳ
  • 2.2 к°ңмқё мқёкіјнҡЁкіјмҷҖ нҸүк·  мқёкіјнҡЁкіј вҖ” ICE В· ATE В· ATT В· ATC
  • 2.3 SUTVA, кІ°мёЎ кІ°кіј, к·ёлҰ¬кі  мһ мһ¬кІ°кіј н”„л Ҳмһ„мӣҢнҒ¬
  • 2.4 мқјл°ҳнҷ” к°ҖлҠҘм„ұкіј н‘ңм Ғ лӘЁм§‘лӢЁ
  • 3 лӘЁнҳ• м„Өм •, мӢқлі„, м¶”м •
  • 3.1 лӘЁнҳ• м„Өм •, мӢқлі„, м¶”м •
  • 3.2 мӢқлі„мқҳ лӢӨм–‘н•ң мқҳлҜё - Lewbelмқҳ Identification Zoo
  • 3.3 мӢқлі„: кҙҖмёЎл¶„нҸ¬мҷҖ мқёкіјлҹүмқҳ м—°кІ°
  • 3.4 м¶”м •: мӢқлі„лҗң н‘ңнҳ„мқҳ н‘ңліё кө¬нҳ„
  • 3.5 3мһҘм—җм„ң мҷң мқҙ кө¬л¶„мқҙ н•„мҡ”н•ңк°Җ
  • 4 DAGлЎң мқҪлҠ” нҺён–Ҙмқҳ кө¬мЎ°
  • 4.1 кІҪлЎңмқҳ м„ё мӣҗнҳ•: нҸ¬нҒ¬(fork) В· нҢҢмқҙн”„(pipe) В· мҪңлқјмқҙлҚ”(collider)
  • 4.2 мЎ°мғҒ В· мһҗмҶҗ В· мЎ°кұҙнҷ”(conditioning)мқҳ нҡЁкіј
  • 4.3 л°ұлҸ„м–ҙ(backdoor) кё°мӨҖкіј н”„лЎ нҠёлҸ„м–ҙ(frontdoor) кё°мӨҖ вҖ” м§ҒкҙҖкіј кіөмӢқ
  • 4.4 мЎ°м • кіөмӢқ(adjustment formula)кіј мӢқлі„(identification)мқҳ к°ңл…җ
  • 4.5 лӮҙмғқм Ғ м„ нғқнҺён–Ҙ(endogenous selection bias)кіј мң нҳ•лі„ кө¬мЎ°
  • 4.6 м¶©лҸҢліҖмҲҳ(collider) нҺёмқҳмқҳ л°©н–Ҙкіј нҒ¬кё°
  • 4.7 Homophily Bias
м ң2л¶Җ | мӢӨн—ҳкіј лӮҙм Ғ нғҖлӢ№лҸ„

л¬ҙмһ‘мң„ мӢӨн—ҳмқ„ мқёкіјм¶”лЎ мқҳ кё°мӨҖм җмңјлЎң мқҙн•ҙн•ҳкі  к·ё к°Җм •кіј н•ңкі„лҘј м •лҰ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

  • 6 1 мһҘмқҳ н•өмӢ¬ н•ӯлӘ©
  • 6.1.1 л¬ҙмһ‘мң„нҷ”к°Җ көҗлһҖмқ„ м ңкұ°н•ҳлҠ” л©”м»ӨлӢҲмҰҳ вҖ” DAGлЎң ліҙкё°
  • 6.1.2 ATEмқҳ л№„нҺён–Ҙ м¶”м •лҹүмңјлЎңм„ңмқҳ м°Ёмқҙ-in-means
  • 6.1.3 мӢӨн—ҳ м„Өкі„мқҳ ліҖнҳ•: мёөнҷ” В· кө°м§‘ В· мҡ”мқё м„Өкі„
  • 7 мҲңмқ‘, ITT, CACEмҷҖ мӢӨн—ҳмқҳ лӮҙм Ғ нғҖлӢ№лҸ„
  • 7.1 мҷ„м „ мҲңмқ‘ vs. л¶Җл¶„ мҲңмқ‘: мІҳм№ҳ л°°м •кіј мІҳм№ҳ мҲҳл №мқҳ кө¬л¶„
  • 7.2 ITTмҷҖ CACE/LATEмқҳ м •мқҳмҷҖ м°Ёмқҙ
  • 7.3 лҸ„кө¬ліҖмҲҳлЎңм„ңмқҳ л¬ҙмһ‘мң„ л°°м •кіј лӢЁмЎ°м„ұ к°Җм •
  • 7.4 мқҙнғҲмһҗ(attrition)мҷҖ л¬ҙмқ‘лӢө мІҳлҰ¬ м „лһө
  • 8 мҷём Ғ нғҖлӢ№лҸ„, лӮҙм Ғ нғҖлӢ№лҸ„, к·ёлҰ¬кі  мқјл°ҳнҷ”мқҳ л¬ём ң
  • 8.1 лӮҙм Ғ нғҖлӢ№лҸ„мҷҖ мҷём Ғ нғҖлӢ№лҸ„мқҳ кёҙмһҘ
  • 8.2 м—°кө¬ н‘ңліёкіј лӘ©н‘ң лӘЁм§‘лӢЁмқҳ л¶Ҳмқјм№ҳ
  • 8.3 мӢӨн—ҳ кІ°кіјмқҳ мқјл°ҳнҷ”мҷҖ transportability
  • 8.4 нҳ„мһҘмӢӨн—ҳкіј мһҗм—°мӢӨн—ҳмқҳ мң„м№ҳ
м ң3л¶Җ | кҙҖмёЎмһҗлЈҢмҷҖ нҳ„мӢӨ лҚ°мқҙн„°мқҳ ліөмһЎм„ұ

кҙҖмёЎмһҗлЈҢк°Җ мҷң мқёкіјм¶”лЎ мқ„ м–ҙл өкІҢ л§Ңл“ңлҠ”м§Җ, мёЎм •В·кІ°мёЎВ·мӢңк°„В·к°„м„ӯВ·м§‘кі„мҲҳмӨҖмқҳ л¬ём ңлҘј мӨ‘мӢ¬мңјлЎң м •лҰ¬н•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

  • 10 Measurement, Observation, and Missing Data
  • 10.1 Measurement Error and Misclassification
  • 10.2 Missing Data: MCAR, MAR, MNAR
  • 10.3 Strategies for Missingness and Observation Error
  • 11 Time, Dynamics, and Reverse Causality
  • 11.1 Temporal Order and Reverse Causality
  • 11.2 Baseline Controls, Simultaneity, and Mutual Determination
  • 11.3 Cumulative Treatment, Lagged Effects, and Dynamic Causal Effects
  • 12 Interference, Implementation, and Aggregation
  • 12.1 Interference and Spillovers: Beyond SUTVA
  • 12.2 Implementation Heterogeneity
  • 12.3 Aggregated Data, Ecological Fallacy, and Multilevel Context
м ң4л¶Җ | мЎ°кұҙл¶Җ лҸ…лҰҪкіј мӨҖмӢӨн—ҳмқҳ мӢқлі„ м „лһө

мЎ°кұҙл¶Җ лҸ…лҰҪ, лҸ„кө¬ліҖмҲҳ, нҡҢк·Җл¶Ҳм—°мҶҚ, DID, нҢЁл„җлӘЁнҳ•мқ„ нҶөн•ҙ кҙҖмёЎ мһҗлЈҢм—җм„ң мӢқлі„мқ„ мӢңлҸ„н•ҳлҠ” м „лһөмқ„ лӢӨлЈ№лӢҲлӢӨ.

  • 13 1 мЎ°кұҙл¶Җ лҸ…лҰҪ к°Җм •н•ҳмқҳ мқёкіјнҡЁкіј м¶”лЎ 
  • 13.1.1 мЎ°кұҙл¶Җ лҸ…лҰҪ к°Җм •(CIA / ignorability)мқҳ мқҳлҜё
  • 13.1.2 нҡҢк·Җ мЎ°м •мқҳ мқёкіјм Ғ н•ҙм„қ мЎ°кұҙ
  • 13.1.3 л§Өм№ӯмқҳ м§ҒкҙҖ вҖ” мқҙмӣғ л§Өм№ӯ В· м„ұн–Ҙм җмҲҳ л§Өм№ӯ В· м№јнҚј л°ҳкІҪ
  • 13.1.4 м—ӯнҷ•лҘ к°ҖмӨ‘(IPW)кіј м•Ҳм •нҷ” к°ҖмӨ‘(SIPW)
  • 13.1.5 кіөнҶө м§Җм§Җ(common support) вҖ” мҷёмӮҪ мң„н—ҳкіј м§„лӢЁ
  • 14 лҸ„кө¬ліҖмҲҳ м ‘к·јлІ•
  • 14.1 IVмқҳ м„ё к°Җм§Җ н•өмӢ¬ к°Җм •
  • 14.2 Wald м¶”м •лҹүкіј 2SLS
  • 14.3 LATE н•ҙм„қ
  • 14.4 м•Ҫн•ң лҸ„кө¬ м§„лӢЁкіј лҢҖмқ‘
  • 14.5 мһҗм—°мӢӨн—ҳнҳ• IV мҳҲмӢң
  • 15 нҡҢк·Җл¶Ҳм—°мҶҚ м„Өкі„
  • 15.1 л°°м •ліҖмҲҳмҷҖ мһ„кі„к°’мқҳ м—ӯн• 
  • 15.2 Sharp RDD: м—°мҶҚм„ұ к°Җм •кіј көӯмҶҢ нҡЁкіј
  • 15.3 Fuzzy RDD: IVлЎңмқҳ нҷҳмӣҗ
  • 15.4 м—°мҶҚ мІҳм№ҳВ·лӢӨмӨ‘ мһ„кі„к°’ нҷ•мһҘ
  • 15.5 мЎ°мһ‘ кІҖм •кіј м§„лӢЁ мӢңк°Ғнҷ”
  • 16 мқҙмӨ‘м°Ёл¶„лІ•
  • 16.1 DiDмқҳ кё°ліё м§ҒкҙҖкіј 2x2 м„Өкі„
  • 16.2 нҡҢк·Җ н‘ңнҳ„кіј нҢЁл„җмһҗлЈҢм—җм„ңмқҳ DID
  • 16.3 лҸҷнғңм Ғ нҡЁкіјмҷҖ мқҙлІӨнҠё мҠӨн„°л””(event-study)
  • 16.4 мҠӨнғңкұ°л“ң лҸ„мһ…кіј нҳ„лҢҖм Ғ DID
  • 16.5 нҸүн–үм¶”м„ёмҷҖ н•өмӢ¬ к°Җм •, м§„лӢЁ
  • 17 нҢЁл„җлӘЁнҳ•
  • 17.1 FEмқҳ кё°ліё м„Өм •кіј мқёкіј н•ҙм„қ
  • 17.2 FEк°Җ м ңкұ°н•ҳлҠ” кІғкіј м ңкұ°н•ҳм§Җ лӘ»н•ҳлҠ” кІғ
  • 17.3 мӢңк°„ кі м •нҡЁкіјмҷҖ мқҙмӨ‘ кі м •нҡЁкіј(TWFE)мқҳ н•ҙм„қ
  • 17.4 FE мқёкіј н•ҙм„қмқҳ мЎ°кұҙкіј м§„лӢЁ
м ң5л¶Җ | мқҙм§Ҳм„ұ В· л§Өк°ң В· к°•кұҙм„ұ

нҡЁкіјмқҳ мқҙм§Ҳм„ұ, л§Өк°ңкІҪлЎң, лҜјк°җлҸ„мҷҖ к°•кұҙм„ұ м җкІҖмқ„ лӢӨлЈ№лӢҲлӢӨ.

  • 18 мЎ°кұҙл¶Җ нҸүк· мІҳм№ҳнҡЁкіј
  • 18.1 CATEмқҳ м •мқҳмҷҖ мӢқлі„
  • 18.2 м„ңлёҢк·ёлЈ№ л¶„м„қмқҳ мң„н—ҳ
  • 18.3 нҡЁкіј мҲҳм •мһҗмҷҖ көҗлһҖліҖмҲҳ
  • 18.4 к°ңмқёнҷ” мІҳм№ҳ к·ңм№ҷ(ITR)
  • 19 л§Өк°ңнҡЁкіјл¶„м„қ
  • 19.1 мҙқнҡЁкіј, м§Ғм ‘нҡЁкіј, к°„м ‘нҡЁкіјмқҳ л¶„н•ҙ
  • 19.2 мһҗм—° м§Ғм ‘нҡЁкіјмҷҖ мһҗм—° к°„м ‘нҡЁкіјмқҳ м •мқҳмҷҖ мӢқлі„
  • 19.3 көҗм°Ём„ёкі„ л°ҳмӮ¬мӢӨкіј мІҳм№ҳ мқҙнӣ„ көҗлһҖ
  • 19.4 лҜјк°җлҸ„ л¶„м„қкіј к°•кұҙм„ұ ліҙкі 
  • 20 лҜјк°җлҸ„ л¶„м„қ
  • 20.1 Rosenbaum кІҪкі„мҷҖ \(\Gamma\)-лҜјк°җлҸ„
  • 20.2 E-value: л№„мёЎ көҗлһҖмқҙ м–јл§ҲлӮҳ к°•н•ҙм•ј кІ°лЎ мқҙ л’Өм§‘нһҲлҠ”к°Җ
  • 20.3 Manski л¶Җл¶„ мӢқлі„мқҳ м§ҒкҙҖ
  • 20.4 лҜјк°җлҸ„ л¶„м„қмқ„ м—°кө¬ ліҙкі мқҳ н‘ңмӨҖмңјлЎң
м ң6л¶Җ | кё°кі„н•ҷмҠөкіј мқёкіјм¶”лЎ 

кё°кі„н•ҷмҠөмқҳ кё°ліё лҸ„кө¬мҷҖ мқёкіјм¶”лЎ мқҳ кІ°н•© л°©лІ•мқ„ мҶҢк°ңн•©лӢҲлӢӨ.

  • 21 кё°кі„н•ҷмҠө л°©лІ•мқҳ мқҙн•ҙ
  • 21.1 мҳҲмёЎкіј мқёкіјмқҳ лӘ©н‘ң м°Ёмқҙ
  • 21.2 DMLкіј м§Ғкөҗнҷ”
  • 21.3 AIPWмҷҖ мқҙмӨ‘кІ¬кі м„ұ
  • 21.4 мқёкіј мҲІкіј CATE
  • 21.5 н‘ңнҳ„н•ҷмҠөВ·мӢ кІҪл§қкіј мқёкіј
м ң7л¶Җ | л°•мӮ¬ мҲҳмӨҖ: л°ҳлӘЁмҲҳ мқҙлЎ кіј нҡЁмңЁм„ұ

мҳҒн–Ҙн•ЁмҲҳ, нҡЁмңЁм„ұ кІҪкі„, л¶ҖнҠёмҠӨнҠёлһ©кіј л¶Җл¶„мӢқлі„мқҳ кі кёү мЈјм ңлҘј лӢӨлЈ№лӢҲлӢӨ.

  • 22.1 н•ЁмҲҳнҳ• нҢҢлқјлҜён„°мҷҖ л№„лӘЁмҲҳ м¶”м •
  • 22.2 мҳҒн–Ҙн•ЁмҲҳ(IF)мҷҖ Gateaux лҜёл¶„ м§ҒкҙҖ
  • 22.3 кІҪлЎңлҜёл¶„кіј нҡЁмңЁм Ғ м¶”м •лҹү лҸ„м¶ң
  • 23 1 мһҘмқҳ н•өмӢ¬ н•ӯлӘ©
  • 23.1.1 л°ҳлӘЁмҲҳ лӘЁнҳ•мқҳ м •мқҳмҷҖ мӮ¬лЎҖ
  • 23.1.2 CramГ©r-Rao н•ңкі„мқҳ л°ҳлӘЁмҲҳ нҷ•мһҘ
  • 23.1.3 нҡЁмңЁм Ғ м җмҲҳ(efficient score)мҷҖ м ‘м„  кіөк°„(tangent space)
  • 23.1.4 ATE м¶”м •мқҳ л°ҳлӘЁмҲҳ нҡЁмңЁ н•ңкі„ вҖ” лҸ„м¶ң кіјм •
  • 24 1 мһҘмқҳ н•өмӢ¬ н•ӯлӘ©
  • 24.1.1 н‘ңмӨҖ л¶ҖнҠёмҠӨнҠёлһ©мқҳ нғҖлӢ№м„ұ мЎ°кұҙ
  • 24.1.2 л¶ҖнҠёмҠӨнҠёлһ©мқҙ мӢӨнҢЁн•ҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ° вҖ” л№„м •м№ҷ(non-regular) м¶”м •лҹү, кІҪкі„ л¬ём ң
  • 24.1.3 м„ңлёҢмғҳн”Ңл§Ғ(subsampling)кіј m-out-of-n л¶ҖнҠёмҠӨнҠёлһ©
  • 24.1.4 мқҙмӨ‘ кё°кі„н•ҷмҠөм—җм„ңмқҳ көҗм°Ё м Ғн•©(cross-fitting)кіј л¶ҖнҠёмҠӨнҠёлһ©
  • 25 1 мһҘмқҳ н•өмӢ¬ н•ӯлӘ©
  • 25.1.1 мӢқлі„ м§‘н•©(identification region)мқҳ м •мқҳмҷҖ кө¬м„ұ
  • 25.1.2 лӢЁмЎ° мІҳм№ҳ л°ҳмқ‘(MTR) В· лӢЁмЎ° лҸ„кө¬ліҖмҲҳ(MIV) к°Җм •
  • 25.1.3 Balke-Pearl кІҪкі„, Manski кІҪкі„мқҳ кі„мӮ°
  • 25.1.4 л¶Җл¶„ мӢқлі„ кІ°кіјмқҳ ліҙкі мҷҖ н•ҙм„қ
  • 26 1 мһҘмқҳ н•өмӢ¬ н•ӯлӘ©
  • 26.1.1 м—°кө¬ м§Ҳл¬ёВ·мӢқлі„ к°Җм •В·м¶”м • м Ҳм°Ёмқҳ мқјкҙҖм„ұ м җкІҖ
  • 26.1.2 мӮ¬м „л¶„м„қкі„нҡҚ(pre-analysis plan)кіј л¶„м„қ мһҗмң лҸ„ кҙҖлҰ¬
  • 26.1.3 мһ¬нҳ„ к°ҖлҠҘ ліҙкі  н‘ңмӨҖ: мҪ”л“ңВ·лҚ°мқҙн„°В·лҜјк°җлҸ„ кіөк°ң
  • 26.1.4 м •мұ… н•ҙм„қм—җм„ң лӮҙл¶ҖВ·мҷёл¶Җ нғҖлӢ№лҸ„мқҳ л¶„лҰ¬ ліҙкі 
л¶ҖлЎқ

  • л¶ҖлЎқ A нҷ•лҘ лЎ  кё°мҙҲ - мқёкіјнҡЁкіј мқҙн•ҙлҘј мң„н•ң мөңмҶҢ кіөнҶө м–ём–ҙ
  • л¶ҖлЎқ B нҡҢк·ҖлӘЁнҳ• м¶”м • л°©лІ•
  • л¶ҖлЎқ C нҶөкі„м Ғ м¶”лЎ кіј к°Җм„ӨкІҖм •
  • л¶ҖлЎқ D R В· Python мӢӨмҠө мҪ”л“ң (мһҘлі„)
  • л¶ҖлЎқ E м—°мҠө л¬ём ң н•ҙлӢө
м°ёкі л¬ён—Ң

мқҙ мұ…м—җм„ң м§Ғм ‘ мқёмҡ©н•ҳкұ°лӮҳ н•өмӢ¬м ҒмңјлЎң м°ёкі н•ң л¬ён—Ңмқ„ APA мҠӨнғҖмқјлЎң м •лҰ¬н•ңлӢӨ.

  • м°ёкі л¬ён—Ң
мқёкіјнҡЁкіј м¶”лЎ  (Causal Inference)

м°ёкі л¬ён—Ң В·

м°ёкі л¬ён—Ң

C:\Users\owner\causal inference\causal inference2\content\chapters\references.md

м°ёкі л¬ён—Ң

мқҙ мұ…м—җм„ң м§Ғм ‘ мқёмҡ©н–Ҳкұ°лӮҳ н•өмӢ¬м ҒмңјлЎң л…јмқҳм—җ нҷңмҡ©н•ң л¬ён—Ңмқ„ APA мҠӨнғҖмқјлЎң м •лҰ¬н–ҲлӢӨ. к°Ғ мһҘкіј м Ҳм—җ нқ©м–ҙм ё мһҲлҚҳ м°ёкі л¬ён—ҢмқҖ мқҙ мһҘмңјлЎң лӘЁм•ҳлӢӨ.

  • Allcott, H. (2015). Site selection bias in program evaluation. The Quarterly Journal of Economics, 130(3), 1117-1165. https://doi.org/10.1093/qje/qjv015
  • Angrist, J. D., & Pischke, J.-S. (2009). Mostly harmless econometrics: An empiricist's companion. Princeton University Press.
  • Athey, S., & Imbens, G. W. (2017). The state of applied econometrics: Causality and policy evaluation. Journal of Economic Perspectives, 31(2), 3-32. https://doi.org/10.1257/jep.31.2.3
  • Banack, H. R., Mayeda, E. R., Fox, M. P., Naimi, A. I., & Whitcomb, B. W. (2025). Collider stratification bias II: Magnitude of bias. American Journal of Epidemiology, 194(4), 774-785. https://doi.org/10.1093/aje/kwae353
  • Bareinboim, E., & Pearl, J. (2013). Causal transportability with limited experiments. Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence, 27(1), 95-101. https://doi.org/10.1609/aaai.v27i1.8692
  • Bernard, C. (1957). An introduction to the study of experimental medicine (H. C. Greene, Trans.). Dover Publications. (Original work published 1865)
  • Buchanan, A. L., Hudgens, M. G., Cole, S. R., Mollan, K. R., & Sax, P. E. (2018). Generalizing evidence from randomized trials using inverse probability of sampling weights. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series A, 181(4), 1193-1209. https://doi.org/10.1111/rssa.12380
  • Callaway, B., & Sant'Anna, P. H. C. (2021). Difference-in-differences with multiple time periods. Journal of Econometrics, 225(2), 200-230. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001
  • Cole, S. R., & Stuart, E. A. (2010). Generalizing evidence from randomized clinical trials to target populations: The ACTG 320 trial. American Journal of Epidemiology, 172(1), 107-115. https://doi.org/10.1093/aje/kwq084
  • Deaton, A. (2010). Instruments, randomization, and learning about development. Journal of Economic Literature, 48(2), 424-455. https://doi.org/10.1257/jel.48.2.424
  • Dehejia, R. H., & Wahba, S. (1999). Causal effects in nonexperimental studies: Reevaluating the evaluation of training programs. Journal of the American Statistical Association, 94(448), 1053-1062. https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.1999.10473858
  • Dehejia, R. H., & Wahba, S. (2002). Propensity score-matching methods for nonexperimental causal studies. The Review of Economics and Statistics, 84(1), 151-161. https://doi.org/10.1162/003465302317331982
  • Elwert, F., & Winship, C. (2014). Endogenous selection bias: The problem of conditioning on a collider variable. Annual Review of Sociology, 40, 31-53. https://doi.org/10.1146/annurev-soc-071913-043455
  • Flanders, W. D., & Ye, D. (2019). Limits for the magnitude of M-bias and certain other types of structural selection bias. Epidemiology, 30(3), 358-366. https://doi.org/10.1097/EDE.0000000000000972
  • Goodman-Bacon, A. (2021). Difference-in-differences with variation in treatment timing. Journal of Econometrics, 225(2), 254-277. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2021.03.014
  • Greenland, S. (2003). Quantifying biases in causal models: Classical confounding versus collider-stratification bias. Epidemiology, 14(3), 300-306. https://doi.org/10.1097/01.EDE.0000042804.70338.44
  • Griffith, G. J., Morris, T. T., Tudball, M. J., Herbert, A., Mancano, G., Pike, L., Sharp, G. C., Sterne, J. A. C., Palmer, T. M., Davey Smith, G., Tilling, K., Zuccolo, L., Davies, N. M., & Hemani, G. (2020). Collider bias undermines our understanding of COVID-19 disease risk and severity. Nature Communications, 11, Article 5749. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-020-19478-2
  • Ho, F. (2025). Regression adjustment for causal inference. BMJ Medicine, 4(1), e000816. https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjmed-2023-000816
  • Heckman, J. J., Ichimura, H., Smith, J., & Todd, P. E. (1998). Characterizing selection bias using experimental data. Econometrica, 66(5), 1017-1098. https://doi.org/10.2307/2999630
  • Heckman, J. J., Ichimura, H., & Todd, P. E. (1997). Matching as an econometric evaluation estimator: Evidence from evaluating a job training programme. The Review of Economic Studies, 64(4), 605-654. https://doi.org/10.2307/2971733
  • HernГЎn, M. A., HernГЎndez-DГӯaz, S., & Robins, J. M. (2004). A structural approach to selection bias. Epidemiology, 15(5), 615-625. https://doi.org/10.1097/01.ede.0000135174.63482.43
  • Holland, P. W. (1986). Statistics and causal inference. Journal of the American Statistical Association, 81(396), 945-960. https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.1986.10478354
  • Imbens, G. W., & Rubin, D. B. (2015). Causal inference for statistics, social, and biomedical sciences: An introduction. Cambridge University Press.
  • Jiang, Z., & Ding, P. (2017). The directions of selection bias. Statistics & Probability Letters, 131, 166-172. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.spl.2017.08.028
  • Ko, K., & Shin, K. (2017). How Asian countries understand policy experiment as policy pilots? Asian Journal of Political Science, 25(3), 253-265. https://doi.org/10.1080/02185377.2017.1353989
  • LaLonde, R. J. (1986). Evaluating the econometric evaluations of training programs with experimental data. The American Economic Review, 76(4), 604-620.
  • Lewbel, A. (2019). The identification zoo: Meanings of identification in econometrics. Journal of Economic Literature, 57(4), 835-903. https://doi.org/10.1257/jel.20181361
  • Lewis, D. (1973). Counterfactuals. Harvard University Press.
  • Manski, C. F. (2013). Public policy in an uncertain world: Analysis and decisions. Harvard University Press.
  • Mill, J. S. (1974). A system of logic, ratiocinative and inductive. University of Toronto Press. (Original work published 1843)
  • LourenГ§o, L., Weber, L., Garcia, L., Ramos, V., & Souza, J. (2024). Machine learning algorithms to estimate propensity scores in health policy evaluation: A scoping review. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 21(11), 1484. https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph21111484
  • Neyman, J. (1990). On the application of probability theory to agricultural experiments: Essay on principles. Section 9. Statistical Science, 5(4), 465-472. https://doi.org/10.1214/ss/1177012031 (Original work published 1923)
  • Nguyen, T. Q., Dafoe, A., & Ogburn, E. L. (2019). The magnitude and direction of collider bias for binary variables. Epidemiologic Methods, 8(1), Article 20170013. https://doi.org/10.1515/em-2017-0013
  • Pearl, J. (2000). Causality: Models, reasoning, and inference. Cambridge University Press.
  • Pearl, J. (2009). Causality: Models, reasoning, and inference (2nd ed.). Cambridge University Press.
  • Pearl, J., Cinelli, C., & Forney, A. (2019, August 14). A crash course in good and bad control. Causal Analysis in Theory and Practice. https://causality.cs.ucla.edu/blog/index.php/2019/08/14/a-crash-course-in-good-and-bad-control/
  • Rosenbaum, P. R., & Rubin, D. B. (1983). The central role of the propensity score in observational studies for causal effects. Biometrika, 70(1), 41-55. https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/70.1.41
  • Rubin, D. B. (1974). Estimating causal effects of treatments in randomized and nonrandomized studies. Journal of Educational Psychology, 66(5), 688-701. https://doi.org/10.1037/h0037350
  • Rubin, D. B. (1978). Bayesian inference for causal effects: The role of randomization. The Annals of Statistics, 6(1), 34-58. https://doi.org/10.1214/aos/1176344064
  • Rubin, D. B. (1980). Randomization analysis of experimental data: The Fisher randomization test comment. Journal of the American Statistical Association, 75(371), 591-593. https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.1980.10477520
  • Russell, B. (1913). On the notion of cause. Proceedings of the Aristotelian Society, 13, 1-26.
  • Smith, J. A., & Todd, P. E. (2005). Does matching overcome LaLonde's critique of nonexperimental estimators? Journal of Econometrics, 125(1-2), 305-353. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2004.04.011
  • Smith, L. H., & VanderWeele, T. J. (2019). Bounding bias due to selection. Epidemiology, 30(4), 509-516. https://doi.org/10.1097/EDE.0000000000001032
  • Stuart, E. A., Bradshaw, C. P., & Leaf, P. J. (2015). Assessing the generalizability of randomized trial results to target populations. Prevention Science, 16(3), 475-485. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11121-014-0513-z
  • Sun, L., & Abraham, S. (2021). Estimating dynamic treatment effects in event studies with heterogeneous treatment effects. Journal of Econometrics, 225(2), 175-199. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.09.006
  • Toulmin, S. E. (1950). An examination of the place of reason in ethics. Cambridge University Press.
  • VanderWeele, T. J., & Ding, P. (2017). Sensitivity analysis in observational research: Introducing the E-value. Annals of Internal Medicine, 167(4), 268-274. https://doi.org/10.7326/M16-2607
  • VanderWeele, T. J., & Robins, J. M. (2009). Properties of monotonic effects on directed acyclic graphs. Journal of Machine Learning Research, 10, 699-718.
вҶҗ л¶ҖлЎқ E м—°мҠө л¬ём ң н•ҙлӢө л§Ҳм§Җл§ү мһҘмһ…лӢҲлӢӨ вҶ’
Markdown нҺём§‘кё°

нҳ„мһ¬ мһҘмқҳ Markdown мӣҗкі лҘј л¶Ҳлҹ¬мҷҖ л°”лЎң м ҖмһҘн•  мҲҳ мһҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ.

нҺём§‘кё°лҘј м—ҙл©ҙ мӣҗкі лҘј л¶Ҳлҹ¬мҳөлӢҲлӢӨ.
